_meta.note: cross-cutting test-infra; completes FR-QA-FIXTURE-ROBUSTNESS-0012's deferred item

title: Convert all remaining e2e seed lookups from ?page_size=200 to ?q= search (page-cap-proof)
business_reason: FR-QA-FIXTURE-ROBUSTNESS-0012 deferred the school-scoped + staff-secondary ?page_size=200 lookups. The kernel caps page_size at 100; tests create rows every run, so once a table passes 100 the oldest seed row falls off page 1 and the lookup .find() returns undefined -> test.skip (silent coverage loss). Convert every remaining lookup to server-side ?q=<code> search, which finds the seed regardless of table size.

param_14.question: Are any e2e seed lookups still page-cap-fragile?
param_14.answer: No — all e2e fixture lookups now use ?q=<code> search instead of ?page_size=200 + find, so they resolve the seed row regardless of how many rows have accumulated. Verified: full suite 44 passed + 1 skipped (the lone skip is the auth-pending isolation test), no new skips.
param_14.answer_source: kernel listModuleRecords page_size cap = 100; the search (?q=) path
param_14.security_implications: none — test infrastructure; improves coverage reliability.
param_14.downstream_dependencies: the searched columns (participation_code, assignment_code, certificate_number, payment_code, batch_code, payment_reference, request_code, publication_code, key_version-via-code) are all in their lists' searchColumns (confirmed: no test skipped after conversion).

verify: npx playwright test (44 passed + 1 auth-skip, no new skips)
